overall_summary: The ElizaOS ecosystem is focused on hardening core infrastructure and stabilizing monorepo architecture while managing community concerns regarding token support and leadership transparency. Recent development activity centers on cross-platform secrets management, multi-agent orchestration testing, and cloud-native monetization features.
key_facts: PR #7197 introduced @elizaos/vault for cross-platform, encrypted secrets management with OS keychain integration.
PR #7375 migrated the Slack plugin into the monorepo, consolidating the last major standalone connector.
Critical headless Linux segfaults and Telegram polling race conditions were resolved to improve system uptime as of the May 3-9 weekly summary.
Operational costs for running an Eliza Twitter bot decreased from $100 to approximately $10 per month depending on reply volume.
Developer 'rma_bot' requested sandbox testing for a Python-based multi-agent orchestrator utilizing Model Context Protocol (MCP) and Tavily.
Core developer 'odilitime' is managing OAuth whitelist procedures for community developers to test new agent orchestrators.
PR #7376 implemented support for monetized container app domains via Cloudflare-managed infrastructure.
The Jupiter integration was migrated to the official API with mandatory security key authentication for decentralized trading features.
open_questions: Is the team still supporting the ElizaOS token, or has it been abandoned? I also noticed that Shaw removed ElizaOS from his X bio.
Did gokumaster64's concern about a potential compromise have any actual basis or specific impact?
What was the specific nature or source of the scam warning posted by dieantwoord1337?

new_issues_prs.title: feat(vault): @elizaos/vault — cross-platform secrets vault + Settings UI integration
new_issues_prs.author: Dexploarer
new_issues_prs.significance: Adds encryption-at-rest for sensitive environment keys using OS-native keychains.

new_issues_prs.title: feat(slack): migrate plugin-slack into the monorepo
new_issues_prs.author: 2-A-M
new_issues_prs.significance: Consolidates Slack integration into the main repository codebase.

new_issues_prs.title: telegram: milady wrapper + @elizaos/plugin-telegram both poll the same bot — race causes silent message loss
new_issues_prs.author: Sw4pIO
new_issues_prs.significance: Identified critical bug where dual-polling led to 50% message loss in Telegram bots.

overall_focus.claim: The project is undergoing a transition to ESM-first architecture and hardening runtime for headless environments.

discord_updates.summary: Focus on multi-agent orchestrator testing permissions and significant reduction in bot operational costs.
Networking and self-introductions by AI engineers alongside unanswered concerns regarding leadership changes on X (Twitter).

user_feedback.feedback_summary: Difficulty reported in implementing movement functionality within bubble-UI based chat applications.

strategic_insights.insight: Founder activity on external social platforms is causing community anxiety regarding long-term project commitment.
implications_or_questions: Does Shaw's bio change reflect a strategic pivot or merely a personal branding update?
Should the core team provide an official update on token support?

market_analysis.observation: Community members cite favorable market conditions but note a lack of a clear catalyst for growth.
market_analysis.relevance: Reflects community sentiment regarding the ElizaOS token and its market positioning.
